name: Netflix
description: Netflix original series, films, and streaming history

questions.q: In what year did Netflix launch its hit supernatural series 'Stranger Things'?

source.notes: Premiered July 15, 2016.

questions.q: 'Stranger Things' is set in the fictional town of Hawkins, in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Filmed mostly in Atlanta, Georgia.

questions.q: The British royal drama 'The Crown' follows the reign of which monarch?

source.notes: Six seasons from 2016 to 2023.

questions.q: The Netflix drama 'Bridgerton', produced by Shonda Rhimes, is set in which era?

source.notes: Set in London around 1813.

questions.q: The Netflix fantasy 'The Witcher' stars which English actor as Geralt of Rivia?

source.notes: Cavill left after three seasons; Liam Hemsworth took over in 2024.

questions.q: Which 2021 Korean-language Netflix series centered on deadly children's games became a global phenomenon?

source.notes: Created by Hwang Dong-hyuk; most-watched Netflix series at launch.

questions.q: The Netflix political drama 'House of Cards' starred which actor as Congressman Frank Underwood?

source.notes: Netflix's first major prestige original series, launched 2013.

questions.q: What is the name of the mysterious young girl with psychic powers in 'Stranger Things'?

source.notes: Played by Millie Bobby Brown; real name Jane Hopper/Jane Ives.

questions.q: Which American actress plays Wednesday Addams in Netflix's 'Wednesday' (2022)?

source.notes: Directed by Tim Burton.

questions.q: Netflix's 'Narcos' centers on the rise and fall of which Colombian drug lord?

source.notes: Played by Wagner Moura for three seasons.

questions.q: The Netflix drama 'Ozark' is set largely around the Lake of the Ozarks in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Starred Jason Bateman and Laura Linney; aired 2017–2022.

questions.q: The Spanish-language smash hit 'Money Heist' is titled what in its original Spanish?

source.notes: Literally 'The House of Paper'.

questions.q: What is the name of the chess-prodigy protagonist of 'The Queen's Gambit'?

source.notes: Played by Anya Taylor-Joy; based on Walter Tevis's 1983 novel.

questions.q: The twisty sci-fi mystery series 'Dark' was Netflix's first original series produced in which country?

source.notes: Set in the fictional German town of Winden.

questions.q: Who stars as Emily Cooper in 'Emily in Paris'?

source.notes: Created by Darren Star; premiered October 2020.

questions.q: The pitch-black comedy 'Beef' (2023) stars which two leads in a spiraling road-rage feud?

source.notes: Won Best Limited Series at the 2024 Emmys and Golden Globes.

questions.q: Netflix's 2023 live-action anime adaptation of 'One Piece' features which Mexican-American actor as Monkey D. Luffy?

source.notes: Premiered August 31, 2023.

questions.q: Jason Bateman also directed many episodes of his Netflix drama 'Ozark' — true or false?

source.notes: Directed 10 episodes; won the Emmy for Outstanding Directing for 'Tonight We Improvise' (2019).

questions.q: Netflix was founded in what year as a DVD-by-mail rental service?

source.notes: Co-founded August 1997 in Scotts Valley, California.

questions.q: Netflix originally rented what to customers by mail?

source.notes: DVD service officially ended in September 2023 after 25 years.

questions.q: Which English actor plays Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, in Seasons 1–2 of 'The Crown'?

source.notes: Matt Smith (Doctor Who) played Philip in S1–S2; Tobias Menzies in S3–S4.

questions.q: What was the name of Netflix's very first original scripted series, which debuted in February 2012 — a year before 'House of Cards'?

source.notes: Norwegian-American co-production starring Steven Van Zandt.
